title: If Shinji was married to Asuka
display_title: If Shinji was married to Asuka
wikitext:
[]: If Shinji was married to Asuka. Post by Rj123541 » Wed Aug 29, 2012 1:05 pm So i had asked myself this question this morning while i was looking at my poster with the three children what if Shinji was married to Asuka . I have a lot of things that i think would happen but i would like to know what anyone else thinks the marriage would be like? Also what the kids would be like if they even have any seeing as how Asuka doesn't really want to have kids anyway...

[]: Post by Der Kommissar » Wed Aug 29, 2012 1:19 pm That depends a lot on how they develop after Evangelion. The characters as they are through most of the series wouldn't be married to begin with: the chemistry, interest, and opportunity is there for a relationship, but each is too scared and fucked-up to try and both are absorbed in their own problems (relationships are give AND take). Assuming they get past their issues? They can complement (ha) each others' weaknesses well enough. Asuka is ambitious and aggressive but short-sighted. Shinji is thoughtful and considerate but retiring in demeanor. What they share in common (parental abandonment issues, veteran child soldiers, issues with self-esteem, among less heavy things) provides a basis for empathy and relation. I'm not sure how seriously we should take Asuka's snarling about not wanting to have children, considering what her body's going through and considering her OWN experiences as a child. Never mind that what we want at 14 is rarely what we want at 20. Or 15, for that matter. Either way, it's the realm of pure speculation.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Aug 29, 2012 1:24 pm While there is a lot of fanart which deserves the caption "It's a uterus, not a clown car!" involving improbably clone-like offspring : Samples  SPOILER: Show

[]: Post by Rj123541 » Wed Aug 29, 2012 1:29 pm Der Kommissar wrote: That depends a lot on how they develop after Evangelion. The characters as they are through most of the series wouldn't be married to begin with: the chemistry, interest, and opportunity is there for a relationship, but each is too scared and fucked-up to try and both are absorbed in their own problems (relationships are give AND take). Assuming they get past their issues? They can complement (ha) each others' weaknesses well enough. Asuka is ambitious and aggressive but short-sighted. Shinji is thoughtful and considerate but retiring in demeanor. What they share in common (parental abandonment issues, veteran child soldiers, issues with self-esteem, among less heavy things) provides a basis for empathy and relation. I'm not sure how seriously we should take Asuka's snarling about not wanting to have children, considering what her body's going through and considering her OWN experiences as a child. Never mind that what we want at 14 is rarely what we want at 20. Or 15, for that matter. Either way, it's the realm of pure speculation.

[]: Post by Der Kommissar » Wed Aug 29, 2012 2:07 pm Misato's ~28, and there's a woman that definitely doesn't have everything figured out. Assuming the world resumes normal functioning (not a big assumption, if you're willing to handwave Gainax's atrocious sense of scale when it comes to things like hundred-mile-high naked women crashing into the ocean and what that does), Asuka and Shinji (and, by extention, the human race) have a LOT of issues to work out. Just because you know of and admit to the problems you have doesn't mean you'll solve them. EoE has an uncertain ending for a reason. Life is uncertain. Would Shinji and Asuka still quarrel? Almost certainly, and probably in much the same way (I seriously doubt their personalities will fundamentally change). Will they have kids? Eventually...maybe. Recall the sort of fucked-up childhood they both shared. I imagine they each would have a pretty hefty fear of doing the same to their own offspring. Employment? Who knows. As child soldiers who saved the world, they should be drawing a pretty hefty pension from the UN. It's hard to say what they'd want to do because NGE Shinji is without ambition and NGE Asuka's ambition is "prop up my ego piloting the Eva, which I've invested 120% effort in." my favoured scenario is the one where Shinji comes home from shopping one day to find Asuka at it like hammer and tongs with Touji. He slinks out unobserved, but a few weeks later, re-enacts the kitchen scene.

[]: Re: If Shinji was married to Asuka. Post by Tankred » Wed Aug 29, 2012 2:55 pm Rj123541 wrote: i would like to know what anyone else thinks the marriage would be like?

[]: Post by AR-99 » Wed Aug 29, 2012 3:02 pm Well they probably wouldn't name their kids Gendo or Rei.

[]: Re: If Shinji was married to Asuka. Post by Fireball » Wed Aug 29, 2012 3:31 pm Domestic violence.

[]: Post by Bagheera » Wed Aug 29, 2012 3:33 pm Der Kommissar wrote: Misato's ~28, and there's a woman that definitely doesn't have everything figured out. Assuming the world resumes normal functioning (not a big assumption, if you're willing to handwave Gainax's atrocious sense of scale when it comes to things like hundred-mile-high naked women crashing into the ocean and what that does),

[]: Post by child of Lilith » Wed Aug 29, 2012 3:34 pm Isn't anyone going to post the Married With Children pic?

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Aug 29, 2012 4:24 pm child of Lilith wrote: Isn't anyone going to post the Married With Children pic?

[]: Post by child of Lilith » Wed Aug 29, 2012 4:30 pm Sweet, thanks.

[]: Post by arkiel » Wed Aug 29, 2012 4:41 pm Probably depends on how the institution of marriage is regarded in NGE Japan. Misato and Ritsuko occupying high-ranking positions in NERV suggests there's been a sea-change in gender attitudes in NGE Japan. If NERV is simply a very liberal organization, and gender norms remain largely unchanged (compared to REAL Japan, which kinda sucks in this regard), then Asuka would refuse to get married on the principle that it would make her subservient to Shinji, which is probably never going to be okay.

[]: Post by Bagheera » Wed Aug 29, 2012 4:43 pm arkiel wrote: Probably depends on how the institution of marriage is regarded in Japan. Misato and Ritsuko occupying high-ranking positions in NERV suggests there's been a sea-change in gender attitudes in Japan. If NERV is simply a very liberal organization, and gender norms remain largely unchanged, then Asuka would refuse to get married on the principle that it would make her subservient to Shinji, which is probably never going to be okay.

[]: Post by Der Kommissar » Wed Aug 29, 2012 4:48 pm Bagheera: when I was referring to a return to bickering, I was predicting that as their more-or-less level end state, accepting the OP's conceits that 1.) they'd stay together 2.) they'd get over their problems. Like you said, sardonic back-and-forth without any real malice. I certainly don't expect a return to the early series quarrels right after they crawl out of the tang. Assuming they stick around each other during that time, that's going to be some really fucking awkward and taxing hours, days, weeks...

[]: Post by arkiel » Wed Aug 29, 2012 5:06 pm Bagheera wrote: Though that doesn't appear to be how it worked out for Gendo and Yui, gender norms be damned.

[]: Re: If Shinji was married to Asuka. Post by Lurkis » Wed Aug 29, 2012 5:09 pm Tankred wrote:

[]: Post by Der Kommissar » Wed Aug 29, 2012 5:10 pm It's horrifying to imagine what sort of name structure would result from such a marriage. Does Shinji end up being Shinji Langley-Soryu-Ikari or just Shinji Langley Soryu?

[]: Post by Bagheera » Wed Aug 29, 2012 5:20 pm Der Kommissar wrote: Bagheera: when I was referring to a return to bickering, I was predicting that as their more-or-less level end state, accepting the OP's conceits that 1.) they'd stay together 2.) they'd get over their problems. Like you said, sardonic back-and-forth without any real malice.

[]: Post by arkiel » Wed Aug 29, 2012 5:32 pm Bagheera wrote: Years. And honestly, I don't think they'd be together much of that time; if they're to have any chance at all they have to be separated and tended to by professionals for a very long time before anyone lets them in the same room together. That said, I think the best part of such a scenario is the wonderful amounts of ammo they'd have to use against one another.
